_doc: Machine-readable campaign-governance policy (TRACKED — permanent policy only; operator ruling 2026-07-17). Live campaign state lives in the gitignored operational registry (see schema.json). Prose rationale: README.md; enforcement: OWNERSHIP-GUARD-SPEC.md + .claude/hooks/campaign-branch-guard.py.

rules.one_branch_one_owner: One campaign branch has exactly one implementation owner session; every other session is READ-ONLY on it regardless of any chat message or review verdict.
rules.one_campaign_one_worktree: One campaign = one registered worktree. Reuse it. A new worktree requires explicit operator approval and lives under C:\PZ-wt\<slug>; it is archived at campaign close.
rules.owner_only_branch_movement: Only the owner moves a campaign branch (commit, reset, rebase, cherry-pick, merge, branch move). Any move by a non-owner, or any move landing the tip on a superseded SHA, is an incident.
rules.read_registry_first: Before any write to a campaign branch, read the operational registry. Never reconstruct ownership or the chartered decision from reflog, chat threads, or memory files.
rules.no_auto_correction: If expected_head and the actual tip (or last_verified_head) diverge, NO session may 'correct' the branch. File an incident report and request an operator ruling. A stale registry must never drive a reset.
rules.superseded_sha_handling: Superseded SHAs are recorded, tagged, and dispositioned (e.g. 'NEVER cherry-pick, NEVER merge') in the registry entry — never merely deleted.
rules.cleanup: Campaign close = status MERGED, entry removed from the operational registry, worktree archived per WORKTREE DISCIPLINE. If the worktree cannot yet be archived (e.g. post-merge follow-up still open), the entry moves to MERGED_PENDING_ARCHIVE — write-restricted — until it can be removed.
rules.sha_authority: expected_head and last_verified_head are ALWAYS campaign BRANCH-TIP SHAs. A main-side/squash-merge SHA belongs ONLY in the optional inert `merge` object and is never compared with worktree HEAD by any guard.
rules.no_deployment_lifecycle_here: UAT, business-owner sign-off, deployment validation and production completion are owned by PROJECT_STATE.md (Business Feature Completeness Standard). The campaign registry is a branch/worktree OWNERSHIP authority and must not grow deployment lifecycle fields.
rules.unknown_state_fails_closed: A `state` value outside state_enum is never treated as permissive. The guard emits an explicit `ask` naming the offending value; it must not fall through to OTHER_STATES.

state_operation_matrix._doc: Operator second ruling §6: the guard enforces STATE, not just ownership. Ownership match alone is NOT sufficient to permit a write.

UNKNOWN_STATE._doc: A state outside state_enum is a fail-closed enforcement boundary: the guard emits an explicit `ask` naming the offending value. It NEVER falls through to OTHER_STATES (ADR-campaign-state-lifecycle-sha-authority, 2026-07-19).

OTHER_STATES._doc: Applies only to states present in state_enum and not write-restricted.

fail_closed_on: missing operator approval for new worktree creation
